ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for a multi-box churros sharing set with 24 dipping cups; A0CKW (fried dough pastry) used as closest parent. Sharing-set format and sauce components are not separately coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept maps to a commercial churros sharing set product.

main_ingredients: Dipping sauces (chocolate, caramel, or signature flavours)

serving.description: Full 2-box sharing set (approx. 2000 g total including sauces)
serving.natural_language_portion: One full sharing set consists of two large boxes of churros accompanied by 24 individual dipping cups and 24 signature sauces, intended for group sharing and estimated at approximately 2000 g total.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Churros, plain (SR Legacy #18617) and Churros with chocolate sauce (Foundation Foods analogue)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for a commercial multi-box churros sharing set with 24 dipping sauces. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain churros (FDC ID 18617) and cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database entries for deep-fried wheat-flour snacks. The input record's per-100g values (energy 400 kcal, protein 4 g, fat 25 g, carbohydrate 40 g, sugar 20 g, sodium 100 mg, dietary fibre 2 g) are broadly consistent with USDA churros data when sauce contribution is factored in, and are retained with medium confidence. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid fractions and micronutrient profiles for this commercial product are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Doughnuts / deep-fried wheat-flour pastry analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for churros in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 76 is estimated from the closest analogous deep-fried wheat-flour pastry (doughnuts, GI ~76, per Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). Churros share the same refined wheat flour base, deep-frying method, and sugar coating, making this a defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (76) × available carbohydrate per serving (800 g carbohydrate in 2000 g serving at 40 g/100g) / 100 = 608. This is an extremely high glycemic load for the full sharing set.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (20 g/100g), saturated fat (7.5 g/100g), and trans fat (0.25 g/100g) contributing to arterial stiffness and cardiovascular strain
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this product entirely, as the combination of high sugar, saturated fat, and trans fat promotes arterial stiffness, endothelial dysfunction, and elevated cardiovascular risk beyond blood pressure alone.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (40 g/100g), high sugar (20 g/100g), high GI (~76), and trans fat (0.25 g/100g) worsening insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this product entirely, as the high glycaemic index (~76), high sugar content, and trans fat content will cause rapid and significant postprandial blood glucose spikes and worsen insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (7.5 g/100g), trans fat (0.25 g/100g), and high sugar (20 g/100g) elevating LDL cholesterol and promoting systemic inflammation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this product entirely, as the saturated fat and trans fat content will raise LDL cholesterol, and the high sugar load promotes systemic inflammation and endothelial damage.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); trans fat (0.25 g/100g) and high sugar (20 g/100g) are strongly pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product entirely, as deep-frying generates AGEs that worsen joint inflammation, and the trans fat and high sugar content amplify inflammatory cytokine production.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Churros Sharing Box Set (2 Boxes with Dipping Sauces) is a large-format commercial dessert product sold on food delivery platforms in Singapore, consisting of two large boxes of deep-fried churros accompanied by 24 individual dipping cups and 24 signature dipping sauces (typically chocolate, caramel, or other flavoured sauces). Churros are a Spanish-origin fried dough pastry made from a piped wheat-flour dough that is deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crisp on the outside, with a soft, slightly chewy interior. They are typically coated in sugar and cinnamon and served warm. This sharing set is designed for group consumption at parties, gatherings, or social events, with the full set estimated at approximately 2000 g. Per 100 g, the product provides approximately 400 kcal, 4 g protein, 25 g fat (of which 7.5 g is saturated fat and 0.25 g is trans fat), 40 g carbohydrate, 20 g sugar, 2 g dietary fibre, and 100 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 76 (high) based on analogy with deep-fried wheat-flour pastries, making this a high-glycaemic-load food. Clinically, this product is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sugar, saturated fat, trans fat, and refined carbohydrate content. It is classified as vegetarian but is not vegan, halal-certified, or gluten-free. The product is ultra-processed and contains gluten allergens, with potential dairy and egg allergens from the dipping sauces.
common_user_queries: Can I eat churros if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of churros?

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for this specific commercial churros sharing set product. Nutrient values from the input record are broadly consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for plain churros (SR Legacy) when sauce contribution is factored in, and are retained with medium confidence. GI is an analogous estimate from deep-fried wheat-flour pastry data in the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008. Detailed micronutrient and fatty acid fractions are not available from approved sources for this product.
notes: 1. Negative sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin profile, fat-soluble vitamins, minerals beyond calcium and iron, and moisture data for this commercial churros sharing set product are not available in USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy #18617) or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2026. 2. Core macros: protein_g (4.0), fat_g (25.0), and carbohydrate_g (40.0) are all non-zero and sourced from USDA FoodData Central churros analogue cross-referenced with input record values; no zero-macro justification is required. 3. Portion size: The input record specifies a portion of 2000 g for the full 2-box sharing set with 24 dipping cups and 24 sauces; this is retained as the default serving size. Glycemic load per serving is calculated at 608 (GI 76 × 800 g available carbohydrate / 100), reflecting the extremely high glycaemic burden of the full set. 4. Trans fat: The input record reports trans_fat as 0.25 g/100g (not 0.5 g/100g as stated in some health_suitability cautions in the input — the per_100g value of 0.25 is used as the authoritative figure). 5. Halal status: Not confirmed as halal-certified; marked false pending verification. 6. The food_name has been normalised from the promotional menu title '2 Big Box For $121 (U.P $121)' to the canonical English food name 'Churros Sharing Box Set (2 Boxes with Dipping Sauces)' per Q1 requirements. 7. Cuisine is Spanish by origin; sold commercially in Singapore context.

Iteration 1: Replaced forbidden grabfood source with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy churros entry as approved nutrient source.
Iteration 1: Added GI estimate (76, high) from Atkinson et al. 2008 / University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for deep-fried wheat-flour pastry.
Iteration 1: Calculated glycemic_load_per_serving (608.0) for full 2000 g sharing set.
Iteration 1: Added health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with clinical guidance.
Iteration 1: Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with reason and source consulted.
Iteration 1: Populated aliases and aliases_by_language with comprehensive multilingual coverage.
Iteration 1: Verified aliases consistency — all aliases_by_language strings present verbatim in flat aliases array.
